review_text: We really enjoyed our stay here.  Cabin was old but clean, well equipped and with a very good layout.  Location was close to shops and Maria Island ferry terminal.  Would definitely stay again if we are in this part of the world.
Not really a caravan park, was empty, had camp kitchen and toilets $2 for showers had 2 washing machine, close to everything but only van park there so we had little choice

Caravan park with everything you need - cook shelter, water and power, and friendly staff. Super close to the Maria Island Ferry and 5 min walk to an IGA. Ideal for a one nighter before or after a day exploring Maria Island.

New owners , very helpful and friendly.
Quiet .
Great spot to catch the ferry or cruise for Maria Island.
Little tired but who knows what new management will do .
Loved our 2 night stay .
Paid showers .

我們租一個營地49元一晚，接待人員很親切，只是廚房設備略髒，女生廁所只有2間，淋浴間也是2間，洗澡是2塊錢有5分鐘，實際上大約有10分鐘，熱水非常燙，幾乎沒有冷水可以調節水溫，沒有提供吹風機。
優點是地理位置很好，晚上很安靜，隔天要去Maria Island、又想洗個超燙熱水澡可以住這裡。
Great spot to park our van on our trip to do the full day Maria island tour.

We chose this place because it was convenient.  On arrival i assume it was Sandy was managing the office gave us our key, she wasn't friendly at all. The cabin was neat & tidy on arrival.  There was no lounge which we expected. The shower plug hole was clog with hair, the shower curtain was so long it hung into the shower,  there was no lid on the toilet.  We wanted to cook eggs & discovered limited utensils, a painter scraper was the only thing we could find. If you need some where basic this place is fine.

(由 Google 提供翻譯) 這間小木屋三年前就已停止預訂，但網站上仍顯示可預訂。即使我們收到了他們的預訂確認，他們仍然堅稱是系統錯誤，並收取了更高的價格。時至今日，這間小木屋的資訊仍然沒有更正，繼續誤導消費者。

(原始評論)
This cabin was discontinued three years ago, but it's still listed as available for booking on the official website. Even after we received their booking confirmation, they insisted it was a system error and charged higher prices. To this day, the cabin remains listed on the official website without correction...

Its a place to park for your caravan for the night. You wouldn't be staying here for anything but sleep. Showers block is dated but clean. Not happy to have to pay for a shower on top of site rate. The park manager was lovely, welcoming and accommodating so service was great. Walking distance to IGA and local pub.

Very small caravan park and very expensive $49 per night (p&w) plus you have to pay for showers as well. Has only 2 x showers,  2 x toilets (ladies), sites are very small, really only suitable for motorhomes. I wish we'd  known before we stayed here. There is a free camp on the foreshore and a laundromat that has showers, I'll stay there next time...
Great spot for a quick or long stay. Good facilities and quiet.
This Caravan Park is small but functional and we enjoyed our stay.However,paying an extra $2.00 over and above a fairly significant price tag for a hot shower-is very annoying!!

Convenient location for catching the Ferry to Maria Island.
In general the cabin was clean except the bathroom needs a major overhaul..mould in the grout in the whole shower.
Everything there that was needed, but no oven.
Didn't havehigh exoectations, but overpriced for the quality.
$63 powered site per night plus $2 coin showers.. rip off
Great little caravan park.
Nice campsite with a cute camp kitchen and toilet/shower facilities! Good location if you’re planning a day trip to Maria Island!

With plenty of free camping in town, including a laundry with showers, we wish we had stayed elsewhere. This place charges a premium for the minimum, and then charges you again for the privilege of showering. Sites are small and tight, bathrooms are mouldy, and there’s been no investment in this park since 1980. The park survives on its walking distance to the Maria island ferry.

The website pictures well over represent this caravan park. The facilities need upgrading. A shower costs 2x $1 coins for 5 mins.  2 showers- one shower head does not stand and the other shower drains very slowly. 2 female toilets for the park. If I’d known I would have free camped and used the town laundromat and shower facility.

Kleiner netter Campingplatz mit toller Lage, nur 10 Minuten vom Pier
Die Anlagen sind teils recht neu (Küche), Teils etwas älter (Bäder). Sauberkeit ist ok würde  ich sagen. Dusche kostet 2 Dollar, fand ich bei dem ohnehin hohen Preis etwas frech. Überhaupt fand ich den Platz etwas teurer.
It might be old, but it's clean and quiet, and the manager is very helpful. Very convenient to the Maria Island ferry.
Loved it 👌 the owners are awesome & the staff are excellent & polite. The park is very neat,tidy & well maintained  stayed 4 nights & could of stayed another 4 very peaceful 10/10
We stayed 1 night in our camper and found it perfect! Clean and good facilities, dump station, and the guy was helpful. Would stay again without reservation.
Great for an overnight stopover, rooms nice and clean.
Great little caravan park.|Facilities were very good|Short walk to the off lead dog area |IGA within a short walk as well.|Would  stay again
It's fine for Ferry access. Small sites but fine for a campervan. Open air camp kitchen. Pay for showers. But it's a 5 minute walk to the ferry.
Nice little van park. Easily accessible and does have laundry (washing machines and dryers @ $5 a load) and camp kitchen with a lot of utensils provided. Overall a well equipped little van park.
Everything we needed for an RV stop!  I recommend it
We did shared curry meal with our travel buddies. The camp grounds is quite, very clean and tidy.
Great little park to camp up for your trip to Maria Island.Friendly host,  clean ammenities,within walking distance to IGA & marina for your cruise. Can’t fault it.
Einfacher Platz mit sauberen Sanitäranlagen. Kann man gut übernachten!
Old place. Minimum service. I (somehow) didnt feel very welcomed 🤧
No hay valance entre precio y servicios. No está bien equipado y es costoso para lo que ofrecen

It’s the only option in Triabunna, which probably explains why it’s been left in such poor condition. The facilities are very basic: the kitchen is open and dirty, the bathrooms are unpleasant and require $2.00 for every 5 minutes of shower time, and there’s nothing to keep kids entertained. If you can, I’d recommend avoiding it and staying in another town instead.

It is everything okay in terms of clearness and maintenance. Shower is paid ($2 for 5min, wish I knew this before). No place for kids entertainment, although we had a great time preparing a BBQ for our group together.
Additionaly, Triabunna itself is not a big deal at all. There is nothing to do around there apart from the cruise to Maria Island.

Great little caravan park. Small but very well maintained. Toilets very clean. Kitchen area great and well stocked , even honey and Vegemite! Great cooking and cleaning facilities. Could be a bit tight on space in peak season .  Enjoyed 5 night winter holiday there very much .

Handy for trip to Maria Island
Very small park but had power and water

Handy to other little towns and the warf.
Kitchen is roomy. Yes you pay for a shower $2 for 5 minutes is very hot and is long enough.
Support the local.

We stayed here with our motor home last night.
Great place to visit and stay. Reading some of the comments about this place needing updating, I love the better days gone, when you have service and cleanliness instead of the brightest and newest. Triabunna certainly does have great friendly service and is up there with the cleanest facilities I've stayed at.
Lovely little town, whilst waiting in line at IGA supermarket (3 minute walk from site) I noticed everyone pretty much knew each other. I like that.
Clean and comfy cabin. Accommodating of the late check-in. Reasonably priced.

We rang last minute in the afternoon as we were  travelling for work and would be arriving really late at night, they were very welcoming and even set the cabin up with heaters on and outside light for us. We thank you very much.
Nice, clean and tidy little cabin, heat pump kept it nice and toasty overnight also
Great location for a great fishing trip. Also, people are so friendly, and the surroundings are kept immaculate.

We recently stayed a couple nights at the Triabunna Cabin & Caravan Park. This is a fit for purpose caravan park on Vicary Street in Triabunna. Caravans and campervans are packed in rather tightly right next to one another but beyond lack of individual space, this park is just fine for the AUD$49 per night going rate.

Reception staff were friendly, powered sites worked expectedly. There is a communal kitchen area with enough space for 2-3 parties to do a bit of food prep and cooking. There is laundry facility and clean bathrooms with showers ($2 for 5 min).

The park is with short 3 minute walk to IGA and Spirit of Maria Cafe on Vicary Street and 6-7 minute walk down to the Maria Island NP check-in centre ferry and Fish Van. All just fine if you are planning a night or two in Triabunna. We would be fine to stay here again.
Mum and Dad like it. Im talking on behalf of my parents. I have not been hear.

We’ve stayed in dozens of caravan parks around Australia and NEVER have we come across a place that charges to use hot water in the shower!  I thought this was a joke when offered $1 coins on checking in, but no, ol’ Mate was serious!  Honestly though, if you’re after a couple of extra dollars from customers, don’t embarrass yourself - just charge another $2 per person!

We were looking for a camp site for the night after deciding to keep driving instead of staying at the original free campsite we had planned. |We luckily got the last spot here for a powered site, and it was big enough for our 8-man tent without issues||We did unfortunately have to pay for amenities such as showers it was two $1 coins for a 5 minute shower.

Had a lovely couple of days in our “home away from home” cabin.|Very clean and spacious. Made use of the full kitchen cooking eggs s as and tomatoes from our friendly host’s garden.|Great location, just a short walk to the Maria Island ferry and the Fish Van for dinner.

Es sollte vieles erneuert werden, die Cabins sind in die Jahre gekommen und schlecht gepflegt worden. Der Platz selbst war sauber.
Die Nähe zur Maria Island Fähre ist super.
Great camp kitchen. Even had condiments. $2 for a shower. Should be included in the price
Our 19ft van was difficult to put on site. Small and squishy. Not enough room to fully extend annexe and car had to be parked 2 rows away.

Great location for ferry to Maria Island. Easy flat walk to jetty. Cabin is spacious, has BBQ on verandah, and comfy bed. Has all you need for cooking facilities, with IGA just around the corner.
All about location here!
Great place to stay! The campsite is nice, and the amenities are excellent. The owners were very kind and even lent us a power cord for our campervan. Highly recommend!
Ideal location for the Maria island ferry in the morning. Did the job! Clean and functioning toilets.
Location fantastic for exploring Maria Island, hosts provided awesome barbecue for Australia Day even cooking freshly cooked flathead. Very accommodating when required late checkout.

We stayed here overnight after a long day. Alan was an excellent host, small, cozy park, which was great as it was quiet. Clean ammenities & the camp kitchen was great. Perfect stop before or after going to Maria island.

Expensive & you only get a small site + they charge to have a shower. $55 for a Tiny site, then a further $4 per day for the showers, so basically $60 a night. Most expensive we have come across since touring Tassie for the last 1.5 months. Camp kitchen does have reasonable equipment but the floor is a serious TRIP hazard, pavers are all uneven.

The perfect place to start and finish your Maria Island experience. A safe and comfortable spot. Walking distance to Maria Island ferry, pub, shops and the Triabunna Food Van. In case you want to cook yourself… a well equiped kitchen is available! See you next time!
Great little park,  they got us in on very short notice,  camp kitchen is well stocked with everything you need and very clean.  showers were great. Owners are very friendly.

We booked our stay through booking.com and unfortunately they messed our booking up. Although taking our money and confirming it on our end, they failed to send it through to the property and mark it as booked so our booking was double booked. The owners of the property were fantastic in helping us communicate with booking.com and also finding as a solution for the night. We were able to still stay on the property for one of the two nights we booked. We stayed in the large family cabin as a family of five. We have stayed here numerous times over the years and will continue holidaying here. Wonderful place to stay.

Perfect stay for a short time. Got the family cabin which has a “master bedroom” and a separate bedroom with 2 bunk beds for kids. The shower has a strong pressure and heats up fast. Outdoor area with own porch and small gas bbq (we didn’t use it).

There was a ridiculous and poor experience, I could not revise the booking date as the system preset the same day, although I tried my best to inform you guys immediately by all methods I can. No one handled the problem and still charged the fee, I cannot understand what service is and will not recommend and come to this camping site anymore.

Great little park!
We stay 4 nights as a big group across 4 powered site and couldn’t have asked for any more. Amenities were super clean, great camp kitchen to use. Nice grassy sites and the owners of the park super friendly and helpful. Definitely bringing the kids back again.
Last minute booking, very welcoming and friendly

Great place. We stayed in a family cabin. Scrupulously clean, comfortable beds and bunks for the kids, steaming hot shower, kitchen range and stove. There is access to a shared laundry. Everything you need for a short stay. The owner/ managers are locals and can give you plenty of advice about what to do around the area.

Everything worked great. Our spot was surrounded by spring flowers, the grills were new and worked perfectly, and the laundry was a nice treat ( $5 each washer, dryer). They also sell laundry detergent for $3.
We'd definitely stay here again.
We had a cabin for two nights.  The cabin was dated but comfortable.
Lovely accommodation,  cabins are lovely
Rooms are clean and tidy. Within walking distance to Maria Gateway.
Nice clean little park. For the size of the site vs cost, I think Coin operated showers was a bit rough. Close to everything.

It is very cramp and not much space unlike caravan park elsewhere, nevertheless it help to relieve the shortage of accommodation especially Easter holidays and it happens everywhere, early birds catch the worms!

This is a fabulous little caravan park. Not overcrowded like sardines, the facilities are excellent and well maintained. A quiet, convenient location meant we didn’t need to drive to anything. Great walking access to everything we needed. We found Triabunna a really peaceful place to stay.
Nice caravan park, very popular so you need to book in advance.

Stayed over the Australia Day weekend with a large group of friends. Some in tents, some in campers/caravans and one in a cabin. Although smaller than some of the caravan parks it is well maintained and the owners are friendly and very helpful. Everyone had a great time. Alan and Sandy even put on a free BBQ on Australia Day which brought everyone in the park over to socialise and it was a fabulous evening!
Just  had 3 nights at park. Was happy, couldn't  help us enough. Had little  spot, so trimed trees for us to fit in.  We have back door on van. Old park  but clean tidy.  Will go back again.
Running down place, live ants in cabins, garden is neglected, not worth paying the price they charge for the cabins.
Clean facilities, pay showers and camp kitchen.  Close to iga and cafe.

Stayed 3 nights on a powered site. Facilities are extremely well maintained despite their age. Small park but this makes it very friendly as you get to talk to almost everyone. Alan, Sandra and Salt (the dog) are great hosts & very hospitable. Camp kitchen is well equipped & we utilised it often rather than pulling our slide out kitchen.

Great wee camping ground! We stayed here for one night in our hired campervan so that we could jump on the Maria Island cruise the next morning. Great service, great wee camping ground and clean amenities. Definitely recommend staying here. Also recommend the little cafe up the road!

Convenient location to stop over for the ferry to Maria Island.  We camped in a quiet private spot under the willow. Friendly, helpful owners. Clean bathroom with lovely warm showers (fab after camping o'night on the island and long walks each day in the rain).  Good camp kitchen.  Walking distance to supermarket for supplies, marina, and take away fish + chips at the marina.

What a cute and cheerful little caravan park. I loved it here. My partner and I started for two nights. The amenities are clean and reliable. The kitchen area is well equipped. The park is close to a gorgeous café, the local servo, and the wharf to get to Maria Island. And Alan the owner is am absolute gem of a bloke. Really helpful and funny and accommodating. I'm keen to come back.

I think it'd only be fair to rate this in comparison to other carvan parks I've stayed at. This isn't an all inclusive resort, so I'm not going to compare it to hotels or bush campsites. This park was one of the best experiences we had on our little trip around Tassie.

We spent a night in a small campervan and had a great experience. Even though most sites seem quite packed ours was out of the crowd and spacious enough.

If you're staying in Triabunna this is close to shops, cafés and the ferry to Maria.

Camp kitchen, showers and bathrooms were clean and working as intended.

Staff was friendly and welcoming and helped us set up.

Our caravan was swarmed by flies when we arrived in the evening and we spent 30 minutes trying to kill them all, and failed. This was just a parking lot on the side of the road.

It is close to the ferry to Maria Island, but it’s very over priced for what it has to offer.

I went by camper van, so I don’t know the rooms but staffs are so kind and friendly. The shop is very convenient. Shower room and toilets are very clean. We stayed here as other holiday park was full but at the end we feel glad that we stayed here.
Stayed in a cabin. Was great. Alan’s a nice bloke too.
Great location

The place has a tired, run down air about it. The units are old but clean enough. There were a few small cockroaches inside. The shower was good and the linen fresh, but overall it felt like we paid a lot for what we got.

We stayed in the Cabin Superior for 3 nights in December 2023. The cabin had everything we needed, was spacious, the bed was comfortable.
The location was quiet. It is just a short walk to the shops as well as to the ferry to Maria Island. The hosts were friendly, welcoming and helpful. We will book the same accommodation, if we go back to Triabunna.
Small spaces, old toilets but clean

Had a good night at this park, it a nice chilled atmosphere. Facilities were nice and clean - especially bathrooms. Shower was great, good temperature and pressure for $1- I think it was worth it 😀👌owners are are friendly 👍😃
Friendliest staff, everything was fantastic. Would definitely stay again!
Good clean amenities.  Showers were hot and good.  Driveway was being renovated when we were there. Nice grassy campsites.
